purpose: Define the first implementation slice that isolates scheduler adaptation signals from frequent gap-policy recheck noise.
design_intent: Separate opportunity-quality measurements from enforcement cadence so interval adaptation reflects real publish opportunities, not skip-heavy recheck traffic.
change_impact: Affects scheduler adaptation inputs, trend insight derivation, and replay/backtest verification artifacts used for operational tuning.

in: Define event taxonomy separating gap_recheck, publish_attempt, publish_success, and opportunity_window observations
Specify adaptation input window that excludes recheck-only events from opportunity score aggregation
Draft deterministic replay/backtest harness contract over publisher history JSONL
Define minimum scheduler unit test matrix for recheck-heavy workloads
out: Replacing existing runtime controls storage model
Introducing new UI routes before signal model stabilizes

acceptance_criteria: Spec defines signal classes and explicitly identifies which classes are eligible for adaptation math
Replay harness contract includes deterministic inputs, fixed seed behavior (if sampling exists), and expected output schema
At least one synthetic recheck-heavy fixture and one opportunity-rich fixture are defined
Scheduler tests assert adaptation stability under frequent gap-policy skips
No changes weaken fail-closed publisher criteria

signal_taxonomy.intent: Enforcement cadence only; excluded so skip storms do not distort opportunity quality.
Represents windows where the system attempted to act on a publish opportunity.
High-quality opportunity capture signal; contributes positively to adaptation score.
Umbrella signal for adaptation windows, excluding pure recheck-only observations.
Policy/diagnostic outcomes not suitable for adaptation scoring.

adaptation_input_filtering.window_construction: Build timeline in timestamp order, then create adaptation windows from eligible signals only (default chunk size 8; replay configurable).

multiplier_model.name: rate_driven_opportunity_multiplier
notes: Uses success-rate centering with sample-size damping (volumeStrength = min(1, attempts/8)).
Skip-heavy windows affect baseline comparison metrics but not isolated opportunity multiplier input set.

normalization: Parse run records into ordered timeline keyed by runStartedAt/runFinishedAt
Classify each record into signal class using decision/status metadata
Build adaptation windows that include only eligible signal classes
outputs: window_summary.json: per-window counts, opportunity score, and resulting multiplier
comparison.json: baseline multiplier vs isolated-signal multiplier deltas
stability_report.json: skip-heavy stress metrics and bound checks
quality_gates: Multiplier remains within policy bounds for every replay window
Skip-heavy fixtures do not inflate effective interval absent opportunity degradation
Deterministic replay: identical input produces identical output artifacts

determinism.fingerprint: sha256 over normalized input events and replay options

initial_test_fixture_outline.description: High frequency gap-policy skips with low publish attempts; validates that adaptation does not overreact to recheck cadence.
Stable recheck cadence but clear change in publish opportunity outcomes; validates adaptation follows opportunity signal.
